message:
[HIGH] fcp-mesh: cap MeshGossip.peer_states cardinality to prevent OOM

MeshGossip::handle_summary is a public lower-level mutator flagged by its
own doc comment as skipping signature verification — callers on untrusted
paths must verify separately, and MeshNode::handle_summary does.

The residual risk: peer_states is a HashMap<TailscaleNodeId,
PeerGossipState> that the summary handler grows whenever a never-seen
`summary.from` arrives. `prune_stale_peers` only fires on an external
cadence (MeshNode::prune_stale_state). Within a single summary_ttl_secs
window an unauthenticated or mis-authorized dispatcher can insert an
unbounded number of distinct peers and exhaust memory before the prune
loop gets a chance to collect them.

Fix: introduce `GossipConfig::max_peer_states` (default 4096) and reject
a summary whose `from` would create a new peer entry past the cap.
Idempotent updates for already-tracked peers continue to apply at
saturation, so normal rotation through a full map still works and the
cap does not block legitimate traffic for known peers.

Severity: HIGH — memory-exhaustion via gossip flood. The cap is a
belt-and-suspenders bound on top of peer-key registration; it closes
the window between summary arrival and the next prune cycle regardless
of caller auth posture.

Tests (fcp-mesh --lib):
- handle_summary_caps_peer_state_cardinality: fills peer_states to
  exactly CAP=4 via distinct peers, asserts CAP holds; pushes a
  never-seen peer past saturation and asserts peer_count is unchanged;
  then applies an idempotent update for an already-tracked peer and
  asserts count is stable (saturation does not break legitimate
  refreshes for the known peers).
- gossip_config_default_max_peer_states_is_set: pins the default cap
  to a positive bound well below pathological-OOM territory.

Verification:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-review-p2 \
      cargo test -p fcp-mesh --lib handle_summary_caps_peer_state_cardinality
  → 1 passed.

rch-exec verification attempted twice; both runs hit SIGKILL on the
remote worker. Fell back to local run per the pane's CARGO_TARGET_DIR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
